2606.25524.title: Cliff Tokens: Identifying Single-Token Failure Triggers in LLM Mathematical Reasoning
2606.25524.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) reach high accuracy in mathematical reasoning, but individual traces on the same problem diverge; some arrive at the correct answer while others fail. Prior work analyzes failure at the step, chunk, or sentence level, or at tokens where failure has already occurred. Neither identifies the precise token that triggers the shift toward failure. We introduce the cliff token, a token where the token-wise potential drops significantly under an adaptive threshold that scales with the local token-wise potential, based on a one-sided two-proportion z-test. Across seven models and three mathematical reasoning benchmarks (GSM1K, MATH500, AIME 2025), cliff tokens act as failure triggers; deleting the first cliff token and resampling recovers pass@64 to 1.0, while keeping it limits recovery to between 0.71 and 1.00. We further introduce a cliff taxonomy of deterministic, uncertain, and sampled-off cliffs, defined by greedy choice and token entropy. Each type has distinct probabilistic characteristics, and the taxonomy generalizes across model scales. Finally, we validate the taxonomy via single-token preference optimization at cliff positions (Cliff-DPO). Trained on GSM8K, Cliff-DPO improves accuracy across benchmarks by up to +6.6. Optimizing at uncertain and sampled-off cliffs improves reasoning, while deterministic cliffs do not.
2606.25524.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）在數學推理方面達到了高準確率，但對於相同問題的個別痕跡卻存在差異；有些模型能夠得到正確答案，而其他則失敗。先前的研究分析了在步驟、塊或句子層級的失敗，或是在已經發生失敗的標記上。這些研究都未能確定觸發失敗轉變的精確標記。我們引入了懸崖標記，這是一個在基於單側雙比例z檢驗的自適應閾值下，標記潛力顯著下降的標記。通過七個模型和三個數學推理基準（GSM1K、MATH500、AIME 2025），懸崖標記作為失敗觸發器；刪除第一個懸崖標記並重新抽樣可將pass@64恢復至1.0，而保留它則限制恢復在0.71至1.00之間。我們進一步引入了一種懸崖分類法，包括確定性懸崖、不確定性懸崖和抽樣懸崖，這些懸崖是由貪婪選擇和標記熵定義的。每種類型具有不同的概率特徵，且該分類法在模型規模上具有普遍性。最後，我們通過在懸崖位置進行單標記偏好優化（Cliff-DPO）來驗證該分類法。在GSM8K上訓練的Cliff-DPO在基準測試中將準確率提高了最多+6.6。在不確定性和抽樣懸崖上進行優化可改善推理，而確定性懸崖則無法改善。
2606.25524.author: Jaeyong Ko et.al.
2606.25524.authors: Jaeyong Ko, Pilsung Kang, Yukyung Lee
